{"type":"task_assignment","taskId":"1","subject":"Implement session command stubs + flag expansion","description":"In an isolated worktree, implement all stubbed session commands and expand flags on existing ones in src/cmd/cmd.zig.\n\nSTUBBED COMMANDS TO IMPLEMENT:\n- lock-client: send lock-command to target client, parse -t\n- lock-server: lock all clients\n- lock-session: lock all clients attached to target session, parse -t\n- refresh-client: parse -c/-D/-L/-R/-S/-U/-A/-B/-C/-f/-l/-r/-t, implement resize (-C) at minimum\n- show-messages: display server messages, parse -J/-T/-t\n- suspend-client: send SIGTSTP to target client, parse -t\n- switch-client: parse -E/-l/-n/-p/-r/-Z/-c/-t/-T\n\nFLAG EXPANSION ON EXISTING:\n- attach-session: add -d, -E, -r, -x, -c, -f\n- detach-client: add -a, -P, -E, -s, -t\n- kill-session: add -a, -C\n- list-clients: add -F, -f, -t\n- list-commands: add -F\n- list-sessions: add -F, -f\n- new-session: add -A, -d, -D, -E, -P, -X, -c, -e, -F, -f, -n, -t, -x, -y, shell-command\n- rename-session: add -t\n\nAlso create src/cmd/session_commands_test.zig with tests for state transitions AND output format.\n\nReference: tmux(1) 3.5a man page. Pattern: follow existing cmdXxx handler style in cmd.zig.","assignedBy":"team-lead","timestamp":"2026-04-03T21:08:24.025Z"}
</teammate-message>

<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead">
You are a TEAM WORKER in team "tmux-parity". Your name is "worker-session".
You report to the team lead ("team-lead").

Your task: Implement all stubbed SESSION commands and expand flags on existing session commands in src/cmd/cmd.zig for full tmux 3.5a parity.

STUBBED COMMANDS TO IMPLEMENT (replace cmdNotImplemented with real handlers):
- lock-client: send lock-command to target client, parse -t (target-client)
- lock-server: lock all clients (call lock on each connected client)
- lock-session: lock all clients attached to target session, parse -t
- refresh-client: implement at minimum -C (resize client, parse XxY format). Other modes (-c/-D/-L/-R/-S/-U/-A/-B/-f/-l/-r/-t) can log "not yet supported" but must not return cmdNotImplemented error.
- show-messages: display server messages log, parse -J (jobs) -T (terminals) -t (target-client). Store messages in a server message buffer (add a messages field to Server if needed).
- suspend-client: send SIGTSTP to target client process, parse -t
- switch-client: parse -E/-l/-n/-p/-r/-Z/-c/-t/-T. Implement -l (last session), -n (next), -p (previous), -t (target session switch).

FLAG EXPANSION ON EXISTING HANDLERS:
- cmdAttachSession: add -d (detach other clients), -E (update env), -r (read-only), -x (width), -c (working-dir), -f (flags)
- cmdDetachClient: add -a (detach all), -P (print), -E (shell-command), -s (target-session), -t (target-client)
- cmdKillSession: add -a (kill all other sessions), -C (clear alerts)
- cmdListClients: add -F (format), -f (filter), -t (target-session)
- cmdListCommands: add -F (format)
- cmdListSessions: add -F (format), -f (filter)
- cmdNewSession: add -A (attach-if-exists), -d (detach), -D (detach-other), -E (update-env), -P (print), -X (no-attach), -c (start-dir), -e (env), -F (format), -f (flags), -n (window-name), -t (group), -x/-y (size), shell-command
- cmdRenameSession: add -t (target-session)

Also create src/cmd/session_commands_test.zig with tests covering:
- Each stubbed command with at least one positive test
- Flag parsing for expanded commands (e.g. new-session -s foo -n bar creates session with named window)
- Output format verification (e.g. list-sessions output matches expected format)
- Negative tests (invalid flags, missing targets)

IMPORTANT: Register the test file in src/main.zig by adding: _ = @import("cmd/session_commands_test.zig");

Follow existing patterns in cmd.zig. Use the existing parseNamedOption helper. When the handler is too complex to fully implement (like refresh-client with 15 sub-modes), implement the most commonly used flags and have others log a message but succeed (don't return error).

After implementation, run: zig build test
Fix any compilation or test failures before reporting completion.

When done, report to team-lead via SendMessage.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Now I have enough context. Let me check protocol.zig for message types, then implement everything.

content.content:
1	const std = @import("std");
2	
3	/// Protocol version. Incremented on breaking changes.
4	pub const version: u32 = 1;
5	
6	/// Maximum message payload size.
7	pub const max_payload: usize = 65536;
8	
9	/// Message types sent between client and server.
10	pub const MessageType = enum(u16) {
11	    // Client -> Server
12	    identify = 100,
13	    command = 101,
14	    resize = 102,
15	    key = 103,
16	    shell = 104,
17	    exit = 105,
18	    exiting = 106,
19	
20	    // Server -> Client
21	    version = 200,
22	    ready = 201,
23	    output = 202,
24	    pause = 203,
25	    detach = 204,
26	    shutdown = 205,
27	    error_msg = 206,
28	    exit_ack = 207,
29	};
30	
31	/// Wire format header for all messages.
32	pub const Header = extern struct {
33	    msg_type: u16 align(1),
34	    payload_len: u32 align(1),
35	    flags: u16 align(1),
36	};
37	
38	pub const Message = struct {
39	    msg_type: MessageType,
40	    flags: u16,
41	    payload: []u8,
42	    allocator: std.mem.Allocator,
43	
44	    pub fn deinit(self: *Message) void {
45	        self.allocator.free(self.payload);
46	        self.* = undefined;
47	    }
48	};
49	
50	comptime {
51	    std.debug.assert(@sizeOf(Header) == 8);
52	}
53	
54	/// Identification message sent by client on connect.
55	pub const IdentifyMsg = extern struct {
56	    protocol_version: u32 align(1),
57	    pid: i32 align(1),
58	    flags: IdentifyFlags align(1),
59	    term_name: [64]u8 align(1),
60	    tty_name: [64]u8 align(1),
